title: sitobolium smithii
side_effects: Sitobolium smithii can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its sap may lead to rashes, itching, and redness. Overuse of the plant in traditional medicine can also cause liver damage, kidney failure, and electrolyte imbalances. If not handled correctly, the plant's essential oils may trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction, in some individuals.
